page: Microsoft Azure DS1 is an entry-level virtual machine belonging to the DS server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single dedicated x86_64 vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1 physical core with 1 thread, supported by 4.0 GB of system memory. Cache architecture consists of a 32 KB L1 instruction/data cache, a 1.0 MB L2 cache, and a 35 MB L3 cache. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 7 GB of bundled local storage.

Benchmark data places the DS1 in the lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both scored 1221.58, indicating weak to poor performance. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 90.2 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 10.48 GB/sec. Geekbench scores reflect this profile, yielding 1012.0 for single-core and 1036.0 for multi-core operations. For database operations, the Passmark database score is 484.06, and Redis SET operations achieve 1.5 million operations per second, both falling into the bottom 10% of benchmarks.

Given the single-core architecture and low memory capacity, the DS1 exhibits significant resource constraints for parallel processing or memory-intensive tasks. However, its dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable, albeit limited, compute performance compared to shared-core alternatives. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a low-cost entry point for basic utility workloads that do not require high throughput. It is suitable for lightweight applications such as static web serving, basic text processing, minor utility scripting, or small-scale development and testing environments.
description: Microsoft Azure DS1 is an entry-level virtual machine within the DS family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single dedicated x86_64 vCPU from an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 4.0 GB of memory and 7 GB of bundled local storage. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers, with a stress-ng CPU score of 1221.58 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1012.0. Memory bandwidth is limited, reaching 10.48 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. Due to its single-core limitation and 4.0 GB memory capacity, the DS1 is constrained for parallel computing or heavy database tasks. It offers a low-cost, dedicated-CPU option for lightweight workloads, making it suitable for static web serving, basic text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS1 is a single-vCPU entry-level virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 7 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads.

tagline: A single-core virtual machine with dedicated CPU allocation and bundled local storage for entry-level utility workloads and basic testing.

4.0 GB of system memory and 7 GB of bundled local storage
Dedicated CPU allocation ensuring predictable baseline performance
Low-tier benchmark results suitable for non-intensive tasks
Best fit for static web serving, basic scripting, and development sandboxes
